revision.comment: Bot: remove [[Category:Sn crud]] (crud category cleanup) [[github:EmmaLeonhart/shintowiki-scripts/actions/runs/23993722414|commit triggered pipeline]]
revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:神宮文庫]]
'''Jingū Bunko''' (神宮文庫) is a {{ill|Library|en|Library|qid=Q7075|lt=library|1=Library}} operated by {{ill|Ise Shrine|en|Ise Shrine|qid=Q687168|lt=Ise Grand Shrine|1=Ise Grand Shrine}}. As part of the shrine's {{ill|Public utility|en|Public utility|qid=Q1951366|lt=public service activities|1=Public utility}}, it is managed under Article 64 of the Shrine Regulations, which state its purpose as "organizing and preserving books owned by the shrine, collecting and restoring literature related to Japanese culture, and providing public access to contribute to cultural development and intellectual advancement."
== History ==
=== Before the Edo Period === In {{ill|766|en|766|qid=Q30564|lt=766|1=766}} (2nd year of {{ill|Tenpyō-jingo|en|Tenpyō-jingo|qid=Q1373181|lt=Tenpyō-jingo|1=Tenpyō-jingo}}), a document hall called '''Fudono''' (文殿) was established in the {{ill|Inner Shrine|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inner Shrine|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} to store texts. Later, in {{ill|1261|en|1261|qid=Q5509|lt=1261|1=1261}} (1st year of {{ill|Kōchō|en|Kōchō|qid=Q1327323|lt=Kōchō|1=Kōchō}}), the {{ill|Outer Shrine|ja|豊受大神宮|1=Toyōke Daijingū|2=ja|3=豊受大神宮|4=zh|5=豐受大神宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Outer Shrine|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyōke Daijingū|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} established a similar archive called '''Shinko''' (神庫). These facilities housed not only shrine documents but also records used for {{ill|Research|en|Research|qid=Q42240|lt=research|1=Research}} by [[:en:Kannushi|Shinto priests]].
=== Toyomiyazaki Bunko === Founded in {{ill|1648|en|1648|qid=Q6941|lt=1648|1=1648}} (1st year of {{ill|Keian|en|Keian|qid=Q1146135|lt=Keian|1=Keian}}) by {{ill|Deguchi Nobuyoshi|ja|出口延佳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Deguchi Nobuyoshi|1=Deguchi Nobuyoshi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q62601522}}, a Shinto priest of the Outer Shrine. It was established with the support of local scholars and priests. The library eventually amassed over 20,000 volumes, receiving contributions from prominent figures such as {{ill|Tokugawa Nariaki|en|Tokugawa Nariaki|qid=Q1323770|lt=Tokugawa Nariaki|1=Tokugawa Nariaki}} and {{ill|Matsudaira Tadahiro|ja|松平忠房 (島原藩主)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Matsudaira Tadahiro|1=Matsudaira Tadafusa|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q10729386}}.
=== Hayashizaki Bunko === Established in {{ill|1686|en|1686|qid=Q7664|lt=1686|1=1686}} (3rd year of {{ill|Jōkyō|en|Jōkyō|qid=Q1146221|lt=Jōkyō|1=Jōkyō}}) by local elders of Uji, it aimed to mirror the Toyomiyazaki Bunko. Despite initial success, it fell into decline until revitalized in the {{ill|18th century|en|18th century|qid=Q7015|lt=18th century|1=18th century}} by {{ill|Arakida Naotaka|ja|荒木田尚賢|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Arakida Naotaka|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 蓬萊尚賢|1=Horai Hisakata|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q28802031}}, son-in-law of the scholar {{ill|Tanikawa Kotosuga|en|Tanikawa Kotosuga|qid=Q6536708|lt=Tanigawa Shisei|1=Tanikawa Kotosuga}}.
=== Establishment of Jingū Bunko === Formally established in {{ill|1907|en|1907|qid=Q2048|lt=1907|1=1907}}, Jingū Bunko consolidated the collections of Toyomiyazaki and Hayashizaki Bunkos along with documents from the Inner and Outer Shrines. The library holds approximately 300,000 volumes, with a focus on {{ill|Shinto|en|Shinto|qid=Q812767|lt=Shinto|1=Shinto}} and Japanese cultural studies.
== Collections == Jingū Bunko's holdings include important cultural properties, such as: * '''National Treasures:''' ** {{ill|Yupian|en|Yupian|qid=Q1069894|lt=Tama-hen|1=Yupian}} Volume 22, {{ill|Heian period|en|Heian period|qid=Q193292|lt=Heian period|1=Heian period}} * '''Important Cultural Properties:''' ** Ritual records from the {{ill|Kamakura period|en|Kamakura period|qid=Q236205|lt=Kamakura period|1=Kamakura period}} ** Annotated manuscripts of the {{ill|Kojiki|en|Kojiki|qid=Q813031|lt=Kojiki|1=Kojiki}} from the {{ill|Muromachi period|en|Muromachi period|qid=Q334845|lt=Muromachi period|1=Muromachi period}}
== Access Information == * Open: Thursdays to Saturdays * Hours: 9:00 AM – 4:00 PM * Closed: Sundays, public holidays, and from December 29 to January 7
== See Also == * {{ill|Ise Shrine|en|Ise Shrine|qid=Q687168|lt=Ise Grand Shrine|1=Ise Grand Shrine}} * {{ill|Kogakkan University|en|Kogakkan University|qid=Q1797584|lt=Kōgakkan University|1=Kogakkan University}} * {{ill|Shinto|en|Shinto|qid=Q812767|lt=Shinto|1=Shinto}}
== External Links == * [https://www.isejingu.or.jp/about/cultural/ Official Website of Jingū Bunko]
{{translated page|ja|神宮文庫|version=104118680|comment=Imported full ja history}}
[[ja:神宮文庫]]
